LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO STAY CASE PENDING MEDIATION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. GOOD CAUSE APPEARING, THE COURT STAYS THIS ACTION AND ALL PROCEEDINGS UNTIL OCTOBER 6, 2026. THE COURT DENIES THE REQUEST FOR AN ORDER EXTENDING THE FIVE YEAR LI MITATION OF CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 583.310 FOR A PERIOD OF TWELVE MONTHS. THE COURT DECLINES THE REQUEST TO SET A STATUS CONFERENCE. IF THE PARTIES DESIRE ASSERTIVE CASE MANAGEMENT, THEY SHOULD PURSUE COMPLEX DESIGNATION OR SEEK SINGLE ASSIGNMENT. (SEE CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT, RULE 3.400 ET SEQ; SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE, RULE 3.5(C)(2)&(3).) MOVING PARTY IS ORDERED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER VERBATIM TO THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/JMQ)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-20-2026 Continued To Nov-18-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Unopposed Motion To Stay Case Pending Mediation
Off Calendar May-18-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DELANEY BARNES AN INDIVIDUAL, ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND ON BEHALF OF ALL PERSONS SIMILARLY SITUATED's Unopposed Motion To Stay Case Pending Mediation.

Plaintiff's unopposed Motion To Stay Case Pending Mediation is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART.

Good cause appearing, the court stays this action and all proceedings until October 6, 2026.

The court denies the request for an order extending the five year limitation of Code of Civil Procedure section 583.310 for a period of twelve months.

The court declines the request to set a status conference.

If the parties desire assertive case management, they should pursue complex designation or seek single assignment. (See California Rules of Court, rule 3.400 et seq; San Francisco Local Rule, rule 3.5(C)(2)&(3).)

Moving Party is ordered to prepare a proposed order verbatim to the Court's ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-02-2026 Continued To Jun-17-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Unopposed Motion For Leave To File First Amended Class And Representative Action Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-30-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DELANEY BARNES' Unopposed Motion For Leave To File First Amended Class And Representative Action Complaint.

Plaintiff Delaney Barnes' unopposed Motion For Leave To File First Amended Class And Representative Action Complaint.

Good cause appearing, the court grants Plaintiff leave to file a First Amended Complaint substantially identical to Exhibit 2 to the Levu Declaration. Plaintiff shall file and serve the First Amended Complaint forthwith, but no later than November 21, 2025.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
